claim: The Codex SDK lets you programmatically invoke Codex within custom workflows, define orchestration in agent.md files, and integrate via Telegram/Slack—offering more control than cloud-hosted Codex.
actionable: Use the Codex SDK (TypeScript) to build custom coding workflows: define prompts in agent.md, run threads via SDK, integrate with MCP servers for browser validation.
source_title: Claude Code Web JUST Dropped but I Already Built Something BETTER (with Codex!)

claim: Build production AI coding workflows with human-in-the-loop: Codex makes changes to staging, validates them via Stagehand MCP (browser automation), user approves, then pushes to production.
actionable: Chain: Telegram → Codex → staging deploy → Stagehand validation (visit URL, screenshot) → human approval → production push.
source_title: Claude Code Web JUST Dropped but I Already Built Something BETTER (with Codex!)

claim: Codex SDK authentication works via O_JSON credentials file (created during `codex o` login) which must be copied to container environment variables—don't try to reuse the local auth file.
actionable: Extract credentials from ~/.config/codex/o.json (or platform equiv) and set as environment variables in containers; the SDK reads them automatically.
source_title: Claude Code Web JUST Dropped but I Already Built Something BETTER (with Codex!)

claim: The Codex SDK's core is simple: `codeex.start_thread()` to initialize, `thread.run_streamed()` to invoke with a prompt—everything else (skills, agent.md, slash commands) loads from your workspace automatically.
actionable: Initialize a Codex thread once, resume it with saved thread IDs for multi-turn workflows; the SDK auto-loads skills and config from your project directory.
source_title: Claude Code Web JUST Dropped but I Already Built Something BETTER (with Codex!)

claim: Browserbase's Stagehand MCP enables AI to validate its own work by visiting deployed URLs, taking screenshots, and creating session replays—critical for trustworthy remote agent coding.
actionable: Integrate Stagehand MCP into your workflow: pass staging URLs to agents after deployment, have them validate changes via browser automation, report back before production approval.
source_title: Claude Code Web JUST Dropped but I Already Built Something BETTER (with Codex!)

claim: You can run AI coding workflows in isolated Docker containers with pre-configured auth (Codex, GitHub), making them deployable to cloud (DigitalOcean) without needing your local machine.
actionable: Containerize Codex + GitHub auth, deploy to DigitalOcean droplets, expose via Telegram bot—now you have 24/7 remote coding assistance from your phone.
source_title: Claude Code Web JUST Dropped but I Already Built Something BETTER (with Codex!)

claim: Codex can create feature branches, merge PRs, and handle the full git workflow when initialized in a repo with GitHub auth, enabling end-to-end automation from issue request to production.
actionable: Grant Codex GitHub token access; define branching strategy in agent.md (feature → staging → main); let Codex manage PRs and merges automatically.
source_title: Claude Code Web JUST Dropped but I Already Built Something BETTER (with Codex!)

claim: Defining your own AI harness via agent.md + Codex SDK gives you flexibility that cloud-hosted tools don't: custom commands, MCP server integration, per-project workflows, and integration with any app (Telegram, Slack).
actionable: Build custom harnesses with SDKs rather than relying on cloud-only tools; you control prompts, tools, and integrations.
source_title: Claude Code Web JUST Dropped but I Already Built Something BETTER (with Codex!)
